#  How do payees upload invoice compliance requirements to Procore Pay? 
#####  General Availability in Select Markets (United States)
![Flag Us](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/eb8f21ce49403a47d7bb5a0cedc7e0e9/7c0ec0977e654cc6bc9f3d8efa80fbe3/flag-us.png?fm=webp&h=14) [Procore Pay ](https://www.procore.com/pay) is available in the United States. It is designed for General Contractors and Owner-Builders who act as their own General Contractors on a job. Procore Pay extends the [Invoice Management](https://www.procore.com/invoice-management) functionality in the Procore web application to handle the payment process between general and specialty contractors.
## Background
To facilitate prompt payments through Procore Pay, users in the Payee role may receive email notifications asking to upload documents to satisfy the compliance requirements for the Payor on subcontractor invoices.
## Answer
If you have been designated an invoice contact by a payor (see [What is an invoice contact?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-an-invoice-contact)), please consult [Upload a Compliance Document to a Subcontractor Invoice](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/about-the-compliance-tab-on-subcontractor-invoices-with-procore-pay/upload-compliance-docs) for step-by-step instructions.
#####  Note
Procore strongly advises payees to communicate directly with the Payor to fully understand their specific invoice compliance requirements. Procore is not liable for payment delays or holds resulting from a payee's failure to meet the payor's requirements.
## See Also
  * [When does Procore Pay send email notifications?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-when-does-procore-pay-send-email-notifications)
  * [What is a manual hold on a subcontractor invoice?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-a-manual-hold-on-a-subcontractor-invoice)
  * [What is a split payment?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-a-split-payment)


In Procore Pay, a _payee_ is a specialty contractor who receives invoice payments from a general contractor. Also called a _beneficiary_. 
In Procore Pay, a _payor_ is a general contractor who sends invoice payments to specialty contractors.
In Procore Pay, a _payor_ is a general contractor who sends invoice payments to specialty contractors.
title: How do payees upload invoice compliance requirements to Procore Pay?
